name: CarpseDeam/Aura-IDE
description: Open-source desktop AI coding app built around DeepSeek, with a planner/worker workflow for planning, building, editing, and improving software projects.
readme_summary: Python and PySide6 desktop workspace with DeepSeek as primary provider, OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini/OpenRouter support, project memory, local codebase indexing, terminal and checkpoint panels, and optional Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Codex worker backends.

name: Hash-7777/HashCortX
description: Open-source desktop AI agent app for private agent workflows, coding, testing, review, local tools, and coordinated specialist agents.
readme_summary: Tauri desktop app with chats, agents, code, workflow, sandbox, agent swarm, virtual OS, source code release, and multi-provider routing including DeepSeek.

name: Localforge
description: Open-source Codex AI and Claude-compatible desktop agent that runs locally, supports any LLM including DeepSeek-compatible providers, and focuses on multi-file coding tasks.
readme_summary: Desktop coding-agent app for local projects, provider switching, large multi-file tasks, and local-first execution.

name: TesslateAI/OpenSail
description: Open-source alternative to Codex App, Claude Desktop, Cursor, and Cowork for agentic software work, implemented as a Tauri desktop shell with a local sidecar.
readme_summary: Defines runnable agent workflows with tools, connectors, approval gates, budget limits, run history, sandboxed workspaces, and DeepSeek/OpenAI-compatible BYOK providers.

name: ECA
description: Open-source editor-agnostic AI pair programming tool with a desktop app and protocol-based editor integration.
readme_summary: Desktop-capable agent can read, write, and refactor code, attach files and directories, consume MCP resources, and connect to DeepSeek through OpenAI-compatible API configuration.

name: OpenCovibe
description: Local-first desktop app for AI-assisted coding that wraps Claude Code with a visual interface, provider switching, run history, and custom agent definitions.
readme_summary: Provides desktop visual supervision around coding-agent runs and supports providers including DeepSeek, Kimi, Zhipu, MiniMax, OpenRouter, and Ollama.

name: CrispyVibe
description: Native macOS IDE for AI coding agents with persistent workspaces, terminals, ACP conversations, browser panes, and git integration.
readme_summary: Targets developers who coordinate multiple coding agents such as Codex, Claude Code, Kiro, and Gemini from a native desktop workspace.

name: strIDEterm
description: Open-source Electron desktop app for AI-powered development workspaces, shells, code reviews, Git, Docker, and supervised coding loops.
readme_summary: Organizes AI coding agents and local development surfaces into focused workspaces, including supervised worker and judge loops across Codex, Gemini, OpenCode, and similar agents.

name: Kaiden
description: Desktop app for agentic development with governed, isolated coding-agent sessions.
readme_summary: Supports multiple coding agents including Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, and Goose, with sandboxing backed by local container and microVM technology.

name: Band
description: Agent-first desktop IDE for dispatching coding tasks to Claude Code, Codex, or OpenCode in parallel across isolated git worktrees.
readme_summary: Combines chat panes, terminals, browsers, code editor, and isolated worktrees for supervising multiple local coding agents.

name: Void
description: Open-source Cursor alternative and desktop AI code editor.
readme_summary: Editor only comparison point for AI coding, useful as a comparison point but less directly aligned with a Codex App-style agent supervisor.

name: OpenSeek
description: Open-source terminal coding agent for DeepSeek-compatible and OpenAI-compatible providers.
readme_summary: Useful worker-agent backend reference, but it is terminal only rather than a GUI coding-agent workbench.
